title: cytisus capitatus subsp. pannonicus
intro: Cytisus capitatus subsp. pannonicus, also known as the Hungarian broom or Pannonian broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cytisus capitatus subsp. pannonicus include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and even show potential in fighting certain types of cancer. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its flowers and leaves can also be used in herbal remedies for their medicinal properties.
